step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment provided by agent1. The primary function of trisodium phosphate in alginate is indeed to act as a retarder due to its ability to neutralize carbonic acid produced from sodium carbonate, thereby slowing down the rate of polymerization. This mechanism effectively extends the working time for creating accurate impressions without compromising on quality control measures needed in dentistry.
raw_response:
I agree with the assessment provided by agent1. The primary function of trisodium phosphate in alginate is indeed to act as a retarder due to its ability to neutralize carbonic acid produced from sodium carbonate, thereby slowing down the rate of polymerization. This mechanism effectively extends the working time for creating accurate impressions without compromising on quality control measures needed in dentistry. 

Answer: A.
